activities_projects.title: Rectangle Area Measurement
activities_projects.objective: Understand the formula for area of a rectangle
steps: Draw three rectangles with different lengths and widths
Measure their length and width using a ruler
Calculate area using Area = length × width

activities_projects.title: Unit Square Counting
steps: Draw a rectangle on graph paper
Count the number of unit squares inside the rectangle
Compare with the formula result

activities_projects.title: Triangle from Rectangle
steps: Draw a rectangle
Draw a diagonal to form two triangles
Find the area of each triangle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that triangle area is half of rectangle

activities_projects.title: Parallelogram to Rectangle Activity
steps: Draw a parallelogram on chart paper
Cut a triangular portion from one side
Rearrange it to form a rectangle

activities_projects.title: Rhombus Diagonal Activity
steps: Draw a rhombus
Draw both diagonals
Measure diagonals and calculate area using formula

activities_projects.title: Trapezium Area Finder
steps: Draw a trapezium
Measure the parallel sides and height
Calculate area using the trapezium formula

activities_projects.title: Polygon to Triangles
steps: Draw a pentagon
Divide it into triangles
Find area of each triangle and add them

activities_projects.title: Classroom Area Estimation
activities_projects.objective: Apply area concept to real life
steps: Measure length and width of classroom floor
Calculate the total area
Estimate how many A4 sheets can cover it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies area concept in real life

activities_projects.title: Square Division Puzzle
steps: Draw a square on chart paper
Divide it into four equal-area parts
Try different creative divisions

activities_projects.title: Area Comparison Activity
steps: Draw a rectangle, triangle, and parallelogram
Ensure they share similar base lengths
Calculate and compare their areas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands relationship between shapes and area
